rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into the target implementation scope if the contract involves implementation-level bindings or subcomponent port usage.
Preserve AADL structure by ensuring all referenced ports, components, and data types exist in the input model and are not invented or qualified with absent packages.
generic_rules: Place component-level contracts in type annexes; implementation-specific bindings in implementation annexes.
Only reference visible AADL features (ports, subcomponents, connections) that are explicitly defined in the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use syntax: `annex agree {** ... **};` and must not introduce package-qualified identifiers not in the AADL model.
Numeric range constraints must derive from explicit requirement bounds, not inferred defaults like x >= 0.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not convert Boolean or opaque ports into numeric comparisons unless the model explicitly defines them as numeric scalars.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
